title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. montanum
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. montanum, also known as the Alpine Oxeye Daisy, is a subspecies of the daisy family native to mountainous regions. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help in the treatment of various health issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and promoting wound healing, among others. Herbal preparations of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. montanum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
